title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Why did domestic abuse reach record high in UK during tournament games? Details here
content:
A strange but intriguing findings related to the 2026 FIFA World Cup have surfaced, linking it with surge in domestic abuse incidents in the United Kingdom, Sky News reported citing the UK Football Policing Unit's observation. This year, Britain recorded one of the highest incidents of domestic abuse in recent years during the most anticipated and widely followed sports tournament.
According to UK Football Policing National Lead in charge of policing football, Chief Constable Mark Roberts, the surge in overall reported incidents was driven by World Cup associated alcohol consumption. Out of 2,322 incidents reported during the tournament, a total of 384 incidents reported were that of domestic violence. This implies that 17 per cent of the reported incidents were of domestic abuse for which 391 UK arrests were made.
Comparing it with data recorded during 2024 UEFA European Championships, it was found that a total of 1,302 incidents linked to football were reported in the UK which resulted in 321 arrests. However, this year football related incidents totaled up to 1,008 that occurred in licensed venues, suggesting it be the second highest number for a comparable tournament.
Meanwhile, during the 2022 World Cup in Qatar, around 123 people were detained in the UK after 573 incidents were reported. Considering England's last five international tournaments, UK recorded the highest number overall incidents during Euro 2020 when the tally stood at 2,345 and arrests at 599. This tournament was played in various countries because of the COVID-19 pandemic.
Chief Constable Mark Roberts suggested that an increase in incidents was observed during periods when alcohol licensing had been extended for late matches. These conclusions were arrived at through findings during England's match with Argentina and Norway, and France-Morocco game which resulted in chaos and widespread disorder in London.
On the night of the England versus Norway match, the number of incidents spiked at about 300 when pubs were allowed to stay open until 2:00 am. Asserting that there is a direct correlation between extended hours of alcohol consumption and reported incidents during match days, the Chief Constable said, “The domestic abuse that we see around tournaments is certainly linked to alcohol consumption,” adding, "The greater the alcohol consumption, the greater the problem.
Implying that there were a large number of offences where the licensing laws were extended, he corroborated the findings with figures. He concluded that increased alcohol consumption "does increase the problems and that includes on domestic abuse".
Mark Roberts alleged that administration's decision on licensing came at a short notice on several occasions which hampered the forces' ability to adequately plan for the potential for crime in the UK. Notably, UK prime minister Keir Starmer's directive to extend pub's license to serve alcohol until 5:00 am came at a short notice, just 48 hours before the England's game against Mexico.
“The concern was that the blanket exemption meant there was no opportunity for local scrutiny to make sure that plans were in place and that an area was saturated," Sky News quoted the Chief Constable as saying. He further noted that the police officials are unable to re-roster shifts and bring officers in time.
Stay updated with the latest Trending, India , World and US news. Follow CJP Protest LIVE updates for Sonam Wangchuk’s health, the Centre’s response, police action and the protesters’ next move.

title: Centre lines up fresh CPSE stake sales through OFS to fast-track  ₹80,000-crore FY27 disinvestment goal
content:
The government is preparing for another round of minority stake sales in listed state-run companies through the offer-for-sale (OFS) method. This move aims to accelerate progress toward its ₹80,000-crore disinvestment target for FY27, according to two people familiar with the matter.
The Department of Investment and Public Asset Management (Dipam) is evaluating a fresh set of listed central public sector enterprises (CPSEs) where the government's shareholding remains well above 60%, leaving sufficient headroom for stake dilution while retaining majority ownership, the people cited above said.
based stake sales over privatisation, allowing the government to mobilise resources quickly without relinquishing management control. They indicated that companies with adequate public float, strong investor appetite and favourable market valuations are likely to be prioritised.
- Government plans CPSE stake sales via OFS to hit FY27 disinvestment target.
- Dipam is screening CPSEs with over 60% government holding for dilution.
- Seven OFS deals this fiscal year have already raised over ₹20,000 crore.
- Government retains majority control in all seven companies despite the recent sales.
- Experts say the OFS route needs to monetise just 3-4% of CPSE holdings.
The development comes after a series of successful OFS transactions earlier this fiscal year, including stake sales in Central Bank of India, Coal India, NHPC, NLC India, General Insurance Corporation (GIC Re), Indian Railway Finance Corporation (IRFC) and Cochin Shipyard Ltd., which together mobilised more than ₹20,000 crore.
Queries sent to the Department of Investment and Public Asset Management (Dipam) secretary and the finance ministry spokesperson remained unanswered till press time.
The government garnered ₹16,885.56 crore from disinvestment in FY26, compared with ₹10,163.02 crore in the previous fiscal year, according to Dipam data. In addition to disinvestment receipts, ₹28,420.49 crore was raised through asset monetisation in FY26. There were no asset monetisation receipts before FY26.
The proposed strategy comes against the backdrop of the Centre's recent minority stake sales through the OFS route. Even after these transactions, the government continues to hold well over 60% in all seven CPSEs—81.19% in Central Bank of India, 61.13% in Coal India, 61.39% in NHPC, 69.47% in NLC India, 77.40% in GIC Re, 82.90% in IRFC and 63.33% in Cochin Shipyard.
"The process of identifying CPSEs for minority stake sales is ongoing. Dipam is evaluating companies where the government's shareholding remains sufficiently high to facilitate further dilution while retaining majority ownership and management control," said the second person mentioned above.
"Once the internal assessment is completed and the necessary approvals are in place, the selected CPSEs will be taken up for stake sales through the OFS route," the person added.
The broad universe of CPSEs being evaluated for potential minority stake sales includes companies where the government's shareholding remains well above 60%, such as Rail Vikas Nigam Ltd (RVNL), Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders, Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers (GRSE), Bharat Dynamics Ltd (BDL), RailTel Corporation, RITES, Ircon International, Manganese Ore (India) Ltd, Hindustan Copper and some fertiliser companies, according to both the people cited above.
However, they clarified that these companies are only indicative of the broader universe under consideration and that no final decision has been taken on which CPSEs will eventually be selected for stake sales.
The government has budgeted ₹80,000 crore from disinvestment and asset monetisation in FY27, significantly higher than in recent years, as it seeks to strengthen non-tax capital receipts while adhering to its fiscal consolidation road map.
Why govt relies on OFS route
Experts believe minority stake sales through the OFS route remain the path of least resistance for the government. With listed CPSE holdings exceeding ₹22 trillion against a disinvestment target of ₹80,000 crore, the government needs to monetise only about 3-4% of its portfolio to meet the goal.
"Unlike strategic sales, which often get bogged down in regulatory approvals, valuation disputes and bidder diligence, OFS transactions can be executed in weeks by taking advantage of favourable market windows. The pace in early FY27—seven OFS deals in three months—shows this is a far more reliable and repeatable route to meeting the target," said Divakar Vijayasarathy, founder and chief executive officer of DVS Advisory Group, a tax and business advisory firm.
However, while this approach may comfortably help achieve the fiscal target, it does not necessarily fulfil the broader objective of disinvestment—genuine privatisation and the government's exit from non-core sectors. The numbers may be achieved either way, but the structural reform agenda risks taking a back seat, Vijayasarathy said.

title: Explained: What is the US-Saudi Arabia nuclear deal, and why is it raising concerns?
content:
The United States and Saudi Arabia have announced a civil nuclear agreement that would allow the kingdom to develop a civilian nuclear energy programme with American technology. The deal, unveiled by the Trump Administration has also sparked criticism over the risks of nuclear proliferation and a possible arms race in the Middle East.
The agreement, known as a 123 Agreement under the US Atomic Energy Act, establishes a framework for civil nuclear cooperation between the two countries.
-Saudi Arabia will be able to develop a civilian nuclear energy programme with US technology.
-American companies are expected to build nuclear reactors in the kingdom, generating contracts worth tens of billions of dollars over the coming decades.
-The agreement supports Saudi Arabia's efforts to diversify its economy and reduce dependence on oil under its Vision 2030 programme.
-The deal will now be submitted to the US Congress. Unless lawmakers reject it within the review period, it will come into force.
proliferation laws and contains safeguards to ensure the programme remains peaceful.
This has become the biggest point of controversy.
two activities that are essential for civilian nuclear power but can also provide the technological basis for producing nuclear weapons.
However, President Donald Trump sought to clarify the issue on Truth Social, writing: "There will be no enrichment of material!"
The final text of the agreement has not yet been made public, leaving uncertainty over the exact enrichment provisions.
The agreement represents a major geopolitical victory for both Washington and Riyadh.
For the United States, it:
-Strengthens ties with one of its closest Middle East allies.
-Helps prevent Saudi Arabia from turning to China or Russia for nuclear technology.
-Expands commercial opportunities for American nuclear companies.
-Advances its long-standing ambition to develop civilian nuclear energy.
-Supports economic diversification beyond oil.
-Gives Riyadh access to advanced US nuclear technology.
fuel reprocessing are technologies that can be used to produce weapons-grade material.
Although enrichment for civilian reactors is typically low, the same infrastructure can theoretically be expanded for military purposes.
Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman has previously said that if Iran develops a nuclear weapon, Saudi Arabia would seek one as well.
Another major criticism is that the agreement reportedly does not require Saudi Arabia to adopt the International Atomic Energy Agency's Additional Protocol.
notice inspections at undeclared nuclear sites and is considered one of the strongest safeguards against secret nuclear activities.
Unlike the Biden administration's earlier proposal, the Trump deal reportedly omits this requirement, raising concerns among non-proliferation experts.
3. Risk of a regional arms race
The agreement comes while the United States is engaged in conflict with Iran over Tehran's nuclear programme.
Analysts warn that allowing Saudi Arabia greater nuclear capabilities could encourage other regional powers to pursue similar programmes, accelerating a Middle East nuclear competition.
Several US lawmakers have criticised the agreement.
Senator Bernie Sanders argued that the deal contradicts Washington's stated objective of preventing nuclear proliferation, saying the administration is allowing Saudi Arabia to pursue capabilities similar to those it opposes in Iran.
Senator Edward Markey warned the agreement "would make us all less safe," arguing it could fuel an arms race in the region.
The Trump administration insists the agreement contains strong safeguards.
Secretary of State Marco Rubio said any civil nuclear agreement would include protections to ensure it "can't be turned into a weapons programme."
Energy Secretary Chris Wright described the pact as meeting "the highest standards of nuclear safety and nonproliferation" while strengthening US strategic and economic interests.
but there are conflicting signals.
For years, Washington sought to link any nuclear cooperation with Saudi Arabia to Riyadh normalising relations with Israel under the Abraham Accords.
After the agreement was announced, Trump wrote on Truth Social that the deal would be approved only if Saudi Arabia joins the Abraham Accords.
Saudi Arabia has repeatedly maintained that it will not recognise Israel without a credible pathway toward an independent Palestinian state.
(With inputs from Reuters, AFP, AP)

title: India enters FY27 with resilient exports, stronger FDI despite wider trade deficit: RBI Bulletin
content:
India’s external sector entered FY27 with resilient merchandise exports and stronger foreign direct investment (FDI) inflows, although a rise in imports widened the merchandise trade deficit, according to the latest Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Bulletin.
The bulletin showed that merchandise exports rose to $45.14 billion in May 2026, up from $43.72 billion in April, led by both oil and non-oil shipments. Non-oil exports increased to $36.74 billion in May from $33.98 billion a month earlier, indicating broad-based export activity.
Merchandise imports also increased during the month to $73.40 billion, compared with $71.93 billion in April, largely due to higher oil imports. As a result, the merchandise trade deficit widened marginally to $28.26 billion in May from $28.21 billion in April.
On the investment front, net foreign direct investment stood at $6.5 billion during April-May FY27, significantly higher than $2.47 billion recorded in the corresponding period of the previous financial year. Gross FDI inflows into India rose to $13.77 billion during the first two months of FY27, reflecting continued foreign investor interest.
However, the bulletin showed that portfolio investment remained in net outflow during April-May FY27, at $12 billion, resulting in overall foreign investment outflows of $5.5 billion during the period despite stronger FDI inflows.
Meanwhile, outstanding non-resident Indian (NRI) deposits stood at $165.96 billion in May 2026, while inflows during April-May FY27 amounted to $1.33 billion, continuing to provide a stable source of external financing.
The RBI Bulletin data indicate that while higher imports continue to exert pressure on the trade balance, resilient exports and stronger FDI inflows provide support to India’s external sector at the start of FY27.

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Govt. has agreed to our request to hold talks today at neutral venue, says CJP
content:
The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) said the government has agreed to their request to hold talks at a neutral venue on Friday (July 24, 2026) and reiterated to push forward their demands, including the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, one of the core demands of the students. “Tomorrow, a discussion has been called for, we are very happy that the government has agreed to our request that it be at a neutral venue. The Constitution Club of India has been chosen,” Sourav Das said.
in at Jantar Mantar over the NEET paper leak, demanding Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s resignation and an overhaul of the education system. The CJP has said the “strictest action” the government could take against examination irregularities was to remove Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, reacting to Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s announcement that a bill providing for stringent action against paper leaks would be introduced in Parliament.
Activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his indefinite hunger strike after the government assured him that his demands would be met. He announced the end of his fast after Union Ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh met him at the Medanta Hospital in Gurugram around Thursday (July 23, 2026) midnight and conveyed the government’s assurances.
Follow here for live updates,

title: Over 6.17 lakh Waqf properties approved on UMEED portal
content:
The Ministry of Minority Affairs informed the Lok Sabha on Wednesday (July 22, 2026) that more than 6.17 lakh Waqf properties have been successfully validated and approved on the UMEED Central Portal-2025 as part of the government’s ongoing initiative to digitise and streamline Waqf property records.
Responding to an unstarred question raised by All India Trinamool Congress MP Sajda Ahmed, Minority Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju stated that 7,99,978 Waqf properties had been initiated for uploading to the UMEED portal as of July 17, 2026.
According to the Ministry, 6,17,102 properties have been validated and approved, while 91,623 properties remain at various stages of the uploading process. Another 91,253 properties were rejected during the verification process. State and Union Territory-wise details of the registration status are available on the UMEED portal.
2025 was a statutory portal developed under the provisions of the UMEED Act, 1995. The platform was designed to manage the complete lifecycle of Waqf properties, including registration, verification, survey by the Collector, mutation, annual account audits, and related administrative processes.
According to government records from the WAMSI portal, the previous portal to manage Waqf records, India has a total of 8.72 lakh Waqf establishments spread across 30 States/Union Territories, covering more than 38 lakh acres of land.
Addressing concerns over pending registrations, the government said Waqf Boards were initially given six months to upload details of existing Waqf properties, with an additional extension of up to six months available through the concerned Waqf Tribunals or courts. To facilitate completion of the process, the Ministry has reopened the UMEED Central Portal for those State and Union Territory Waqf Boards that have received such extensions.

title: Govt. agreed to our request to hold talks on July 24 at neutral venue, says CJP
content:
The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) said the government has agreed to their request to hold talks at a neutral venue on Friday (July 24, 2026) and reiterated to push forward their demands, including the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, one of the core demands of the students.
The discussions will take place at the Constitution Club of India.The development came after activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day hunger strike at Medanat Hospital in the presence of Union Ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh at a hospital.
CJP Chief Spokesperson Saurav Das told reporters that a discussion has been called for and welcomed the government's decision to hold it at a neutral venue.
"Tomorrow [July 24, 2026], a discussion has been called for, we are very happy that the government has agreed to our request that it be at a neutral venue. The Constitution Club of India has been chosen," Mr. Das said.
CJP spokesperson Vaishnavi Gaur said, "The government has agreed to meet us at a neutral place. We will be putting our demands, which we have been consistent with from the first day. The four demands: Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation. Second is compensation for the families... All the FIRs on innocent protesters should be lifted."
CJP activist Abhijeet Dipke on Friday (July 24, 2026) said Sonam Wangchuk's life was "far too precious to this country" after he ended his 26-day hunger strike and hailed his extraordinary courage and sacrifice. He noted that Sonam Wangchuk "put his own life in danger" for "awakening the conscience of an entire nation."
In a post on X, Mr. Dipke said, "We are relieved and grateful that Sonam sir has ended his hunger strike after 26 days. Thank you, sir, for your extraordinary courage and sacrifice. By putting your own life on the line, you awakened the conscience of an entire nation. Your life is far too precious to this country."

title: Jantar Mantar protest updates: Wangchuk breaks 26-day fast; relieved, says CJP founder Dipke, but vows to continue protest till Pradhan resigns
content:
Activist Sonam Wangchuk has broken his fast, announced Leh Apex Body co-convenor Cherring Dorjay Lakruk late on Thursday (July 23, 2026). Mr Wangchuk broke the fast in the presence of two Union Ministers on certain conditions, he said. The government gave a written assurance that cases against students will be taken back if they are not violent, he added. Also, the government gave a written assurance that they will consider compensation for NEET aspirants who took their lives, he said.
Also late on July 23, 2026, Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday (July 23, 2026), through his social media posts, sought to address student concerns on NEET paper leaks. He said, “I am deeply aware that a paper leak is no ordinary lapse; it is an injustice that inflicts severe distress upon millions of aspiring students and their families. It is in response to this gravity that we have executed swift, uncompromising measures over the past two and a half months. The offenders have been arrested, and they now stand behind bars.
Earlier in the day, Mr. Modi announced fast-track courts to ensure swift punishment for paper leaks. The Delhi High Court, in another late night development, designated Judge Anu Grover Baliga as Special Judge of the fast-track court to deal with criminal cases arising out of paper leaks and the use of unfair means in public examinations.
Meanwhile, a government source said a delegation of the Cockroach Janta Party will meet with Ministers on Friday (July 24, 2026), sources said. The meeting would take place at 12.30 p.m., but its venue has not been disclosed. The CJP is yet to comment on this.
This live is now closed

title: Utilisation Certificates of ₹92, 132.75 crore yet to be received by office, says CAG report, 2024-25 in Bihar
content:
On the penultimate day of the ongoing five day monsoon session of the Bihar legislature, report of the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India on Bihar for the year 2024-25 was laid in State assembly on Thursday (July 23, 2026) by Finance Minister and Deputy Chief Minister Bijendra Prasad Yadav which highlighted that 62,632 outstanding Utilization Certificate (UC) of ₹92,132.75 crore were yet to be received by the Bihar office as on March 31, 2025 and the top five departments of the state government with the maximum amount of outstanding UCs are Health, Rural Development, Urban Development, Education and Panchayati Raj.
“62,632 outstanding Utilisation Certificates (UCs) of ₹ 92,132.75 crore were yet to be received by the Principal Accountant General (Accounts & Entitlements), Bihar as on March 31, 2025”, said the CAG report under the title of “Financial Reporting Practices”, which was laid on the floor of the state assembly on Thursday (July 23).
The top five departments with the maximum amount of UCs during the period are Health, Rural Development, Urban Development, Education and Panchayati Raj. “Since non-submission of UCs is fraught with the risk of misutilisation, the state government must monitor this aspect closely and hold the persons concerned accountable for submission of UCs on time”, said the report, adding that though “the matter was raised with the Finance Department of the Bihar government, but till January 2026, the reply is awaited”.
The report under the “Budgetary Management” section said that “during the financial year 2024-25, out of the total budget of the state (₹ 3,64, 518.87 crore), only ₹2, 87, 178.49 crore (78.78% of the total budget) was spent, leading to savings of ₹ 77, 340.38 crore. Out of the total savings, an amount of ₹10,388.35 crore (only 13.43%) was surrendered”, and asserted further that, “this indicated unrealistic projections and deficient alignment between budget and expenditure”. “Although, the Budget estimate was enhanced by 28.81% through Supplementary Budgets but the expenditures remained only 1.48% above the original budget in Financial Year 2024-25”, stated the report.
Under section of MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act) in Bihar the report flagged that “in the absence of any baseline survey during 2019-24, there was no mechanism available with the department to assess the quantum and timing demand for work at the Gram Panchayat level, as well as any local changes in pattern of livelihoods and opportunities for work”. It further highlighted that “against 13,798 sanctioned posts for the implementation of MGNREGA in Bihar, 6,086 posts (44%) were vacant. The extent of vacancies ranged from 16 % in the case of District Programme Officers (DPOs) to 80% for computer operators”. “As of March 2024, ₹ 36.22 crore was lying unadjusted advance against various implementing agencies for more than five years”, it said further.
“The State government had not framed any rules regarding the frequency, place and procedure of the meetings of the State Employment Guarantee Council (SEGC) established. In the absence of such rules, the SEGC held only three meetings during 2021-23, indicating insufficient monitoring of the scheme at the state level”, said the CAG report.
Under “construction, raising and strengthening of embankments under the Water Resources Department” section, the report said that “due to construction of embankments in patches on account of non-availability of land along Jhim Jamura and Banke rivers under Adhwara basin, the intended benefit of protection of 5.26 lakh population and 17,400 ha [hectare] agricultural land was not achieved, rendering ₹28.97 crore unfruitful (December 2024)”. Similarly, the report stated that “the education department did not assess and pay the service charge in respect of its 161 buildings for the period 1995 to 2023 within the stipulated time, resulting in an avoidable expenditure of ₹ 8.47 crore towards penal interest”.
Under “Implementation of “Har Ghar Nal Ka Jal Yojna” in Bihar, the report underlined that “non/ inadequate baseline surveys led to unreliable estimates for targeting and coverage of households under the scheme. PHED (Public Health Engineering Department) could not cover 11.22 lakh households under 46,633 rural wards, including 8.07 lakh households in quality-affected wards. “In five test-checked Public Health divisions, ₹2.31 crore was paid to contractors for works that had actually not been executed”, it wondered.
Nonetheless, the state’s Deputy Chief Minister, who is also the state’s Finance and Commercial Taxes Minister, Bijendra Prasad Yadav had reiterated that there was “no paucity of funds” in the state.

title: NML capacity-building programme begins at AU; ₹3.10 crore sanctioned for AP libraries
content:
The 56th Capacity Building Programme of the National Mission on Libraries (NML) for public library personnel began on Thursday at the Kodandaramaiah Auditorium of Andhra University, organised by the Department of Library and Information Science. The five-day training, which will conclude on July 27, is bringing together public library professionals from Andhra Pradesh, Telangana and Chhattisgarh.
Speaking to The Hindu, Mr. Dipanjan Chatterjee, FA, NML, said the mission was launched in 2014 by the then president, Pranab Mukherjee, on the recommendation of the National Knowledge Commission. “It has four components: setting up model public libraries across the country, conducting qualitative and quantitative surveys of public libraries, organising capacity-building programmes for library professionals, and developing the National Virtual Library of India, now known as the Indian Culture Portal,” he said, adding that the first three components were implemented by the Raja Rammohun Roy Library Foundation, while the Culture Portal was managed by IIT Bombay.
Mr. Chatterjee said 55 such programmes had been completed so far, training more than 2,500 professionals. The current session, he noted, would focus on KOHA, a free and open-source library automation software now used by institutions including the National Library in Kolkata, besides sessions on improving services for Divyangjan, deploying artificial intelligence and ICT tools, and bookbinding, which he described as “a dying profession but remains essential for preserving library resources scientifically.”
Delivering the inaugural address, Vice-Chancellor G.P. Raja Sekhar observed that while library culture had undergone a revolution globally, Indian institutions were yet to catch up with international standards, both in technology adoption and in the upkeep of infrastructure. He called for greater efforts to draw students to libraries beyond those preparing for competitive examinations, stressing, “library is not just for people who will prepare for competitive exams. Library is for all.”
On funding, Mr. Chatterjee explained that since libraries were a State subject under the Seventh Schedule, Central assistance was released only on the recommendation of the State Library Planning Committee, in a 75:25 Centre-State ratio. For Andhra Pradesh, he said, ₹2.23 crore had been sanctioned for the State Central Library at Amaravati and ₹87 lakh for the Sri Gowthami Regional Library at Rajamahendravaram. While the entire amount for the latter had been released, the Centre was still awaiting the State’s matching share for the Amaravati library.
Participants will also visit reputed public and university libraries during the programme as part of the training module.

title: DRDO flight test of ‘Kusha’ takes off
content:
2 min readNew DelhiJul 24, 2026 03:59 AM IST
range air defence capabilities, the Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO) Thursday successfully conducted the first flight test of the ‘Kusha’ Long-Range Surface-to-Air Missile (SAM), at the APJ Abdul Kalam Island off the coast of Odisha.
range SAM typically has a range of over 200 km. India operates Russian S-400 Triumf, which is understood to be capable of hitting targets up to 400 km away.
In a statement, the Ministry of Defence said that this maiden test was conducted against an electronic target simulating a high-speed, high-altitude aerial threat, which the missile system successfully intercepted.
an indigenous long-range SAM system — to protect strategic military and civilian assets and infrastructure from a range of aerial threats, including fighter jets, missiles, unmanned aerial vehicles and large enemy aircraft within a wide range and altitude envelope.
All weapon system elements, including the missiles, radars, command and control centre, have been developed by various DRDO laboratories and industry partners.
Calling the test’s success an important milestone in Indian defence R&D, Defence Minister Rajnath Singh said that it will also eliminate India’s import dependency for such systems.
Last year, the DRDO successfully conducted maiden flight tests of the Integrated Air Defence Weapon System (IADWS) — a multi-layered air defence system comprising all indigenous Quick Reaction Surface to Air Missiles (QRSAM), Advanced Very Short Range Air Defence System (VSHORADS) missiles, and a high-power laser-based Directed Energy Weapon (DEW).
Story continues below this ad
Together, Kusha and the IADWS are understood to be major steps towards bringing India closer towards developing a home-grown nationwide security shield from multi-domain enemy attacks under Mission Sudarshan Chakra in the next 10 years.
Defence Secretary and Secretary, Department of Defence R&D, and Chairman, DRDO, Rajesh Kumar Singh, monitored the launch and congratulated the team members, the statement noted.

title: ‘No debate till Dharmendra Pradhan resigns’: Opposition unites over CJP protests
content:
2 min readUpdated: Jul 23, 2026 01:05 PM IST
Congress MP KC Venugopal on Thursday said that all the Opposition parties have decided that they would not engage in any discussion before Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan resigns. “We have conveyed this to Speaker Om Birla, he said. Except AAP and DMK, all parties had agreed to the Opposition stance.
The Opposition’s decision comes amid the students’ protests raging across the country demanding Pradhan’s resignation over the NEET paper leaks.
The government, however, said it wants to discuss the matter in Parliament. Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju said: “The government is ready for a discussion on the NEET issue.”
Soon after the Opposition MPs staged a protest on the Parliament premises on Thursday, BJP national president Nitin Nabin said: “The entire opposition, including Congress, is running away from discussion, because they know that as soon as they come to the floor of the House, their double standards will be completely exposed before the people of the country and the youth. We issue an open challenge to the entire opposition. Come, let us have an open discussion on every issue related to students and youth, including NEET, on the floor of the House. The NDA government is fully prepared to engage in dialogue on every subject and answer every question.”
“But Rahul Gandhi and the opposition’s political history has been one of avoiding discussion. That is why, instead of facing debate in the House, they run away from it,” he added.

title: U.S. Pentagon awards Oracle nearly $7 billion deal in latest software consolidation push
content:
The Pentagon announced on Thursday a nearly $7 billion, up-to-10-year agreement with Oracle to consolidate the department’s on-premises software licenses into a single contract, the latest move by the Pentagon’s technology chief to cut costs by eliminating fragmented purchasing.
The Enterprise Software Agreement, negotiated by the Department of the Navy, is between the Pentagon and Oracle for its on-premises compute usage.
The agreement spans five years, with a further five-year option, and covers the entire Pentagon, along with the U.S. Coast Guard and the intelligence community.
“By fundamentally improving how we procure on-premises Oracle capabilities, we are driving at least $441 million in taxpayer savings while rapidly and effectively serving our warfighters,” Pentagon Chief Information Officer Kirsten Davies said. The Oracle agreement builds on a similar effort Davies’ office struck with Microsoft in May. That five-year, $9.69 billion deal consolidated Microsoft and other enterprise software licenses scattered across the military services, the intelligence community and the Coast Guard into a single contract.
The two deals mark a broader push under Davies to rein in years of duplicative software spending across the military by replacing piecemeal, service-by-service contracts with enterprise-wide agreements.

title: AMD to invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic: Report
content:
Advanced Micro Devices has signed a deal with artificial intelligence startup Anthropic for tens of billions of dollars worth of AI servers and will invest up to $5 billion in the company, the Wall Street Journal reported on Tuesday.
The agreement covers up to 2 gigawatts of AMD’s next-generation Instinct MI450 chips, with deliveries set to begin in the first half of 2027, the report said.
Industry executives have said 1 gigawatt of computing power, enough to power roughly 750,000 U.S. homes, can cost around $50 billion.
Anthropic will use the chips in its own data centres and through leased capacity from cloud providers, according to the report. Reuters could not immediately verify the report. AMD did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment, while Anthropic said it would release a statement soon.
The deal marks a significant win for AMD as the chipmaker seeks to challenge Nvidia’s dominance in the fast-growing AI hardware market and expand its roster of major AI customers.

title: Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking badminton tournament results
content:
Uttarakhand’s Dhruv Negi beat Rajasthan’s Pranay Katta 20-18, 14-16, 15-11 in the final to clinch the Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking badminton tournament men’s singles title at the Jawaharlal Nehru indoor stadium here on Thursday.
The 20-year-old’s triumph was his maiden senior men’s singles title.
In the women’s final, Haryana’s Jiya Rawat defeated Andhra Pradesh’s M. Tanishq 15-12, 15-10 to claim the title.
The tournament also threw up a few surprises. Tamil Nadu’s mixed doubles specialist K. Sathish Kumar suffered a quarterfinal exit to an unseeded Kerala pair. Reigning Asian u-17 champion S.R. Diksha and Sadhana Fenelon, a bronze medallist at last week’s National Ranking tournament in Kochi, also bowed out early in the women’s singles.
reigning National men’s singles champion Rithvik Sanjeevi and former junior World No. 1 Sankar Muthusamy - who were away on international duty.
Former Tamil Nadu Cricket Association (TNCA) president Ashok Sigamani and Tamil Nadu Badminton Association (TNBA) secretary Ve. Arunachalam presented the prizes.
Other results (finals): Doubles: Men: Viplav Kuvale and Viraj Kuvale (Mah) bt Mohammad Amaan (Raj) and Dingku Singh Konthoujam (Man) 13-15, 15-4, 15-4; Women: V.S. Varshini (TN) and Nayana S. Oasis (Ker) bt Rutaparna Panda and Swetaparna Panda (Odi) 15-12, 15-6; Mixed: Vimalraj Annadurai and Shreya Balaji (TN) bt Sankarprasad Udayakumar and Diya Arun (Ker) 15-8, 15-10.

title: Rajpal realistic about India’s medal chances in curtailed CWG
content:
As Chef de Mission of the Indian contingent at the Commonwealth Games here, Rohit Rajpal has his hands full. But the 55-year-old, who is also the vice-president of the All India Tennis Association, has assembled a team to ensure everything runs smoothly.
In fact, he has set up a dedicated office to make things easier for both athletes and the media.
“Weather reports are predicting rain. It’s windy as well, which could affect the performance of athletes in certain disciplines. We are trying to understand the local conditions so that we can prepare accordingly,” Rajpal told The Hindu here on Wednesday night.
On expectations from the Indian contingent, Rajpal said it was important to keep them realistic, given that this was not a “normal” edition of the Games.
“These Games were originally supposed to be held in Victoria (Australia), but they got cancelled. Glasgow had stepped in and is hosting them in a limited format. We have only 10 sports. We can expect medals in athletics, weightlifting, boxing and lawn bowls and a few events. Our para athletes are also looking to contribute,” he said.
Rajpal believes the Glasgow Games carry added significance as Ahmedabad is set to host the 2030 edition and will officially receive the baton during the closing ceremony.
“Symbolically, these Games are very important because India is going to be the next host. The Gujarat government team is here, and there is knowledge transfer happening during the Games as well,” he said.
At the closing ceremony, Rajpal said, India will showcase its culture to the world.
“We will have a dance and cultural presentation that will offer a glimpse of what India and Gujarat have in store for the 2030 Games in Ahmedabad. Gujarat government has set up an Ahmedabad Experience Centre here. Members of the Indian diaspora from across the world have been invited,” Rajpal added.

title: The ASEAN Championship: A regional tournament that became a cultural institution
content:
A few days ago, Prime Minister Narendra Modi was in Jakarta speaking to the Indian community. He talked about the FIFA World Cup, joking that every time he visits Indonesia, football fever seems to be in the air.
Indonesia is one of those places where football is discussed with the seriousness of politics and the emotion of a religion. While his reference was to highlight that he always visits Indonesia during a World Cup year, he was certainly briefed about the region’s passionate support for the game.
The final between Spain and Argentina has barely faded from television screens, while another tournament – the ASEAN Championship – is preparing to begin almost unnoticed outside the region. It is set to run from July 24 to August 26.
The ASEAN Championship is now thirty years old. When it began in 1996, it was a modest regional competition. Today it involves ten teams, and as many stadiums, across nine countries, a home-and-away group stage, and a level of fan engagement many larger football markets would envy.
READ | Spain refines its style, rekindles winning spree
The opening match this year will see Cambodia take on Singapore in Phnom Penh. If you had asked a football executive in Europe 20 years ago which country would host the largest stadium in an ASEAN tournament, Cambodia would not have been high on the list. And that has changed now, with Morodok Techo National Stadium capable of hosting 60,000 people – something that would’ve seemed improbable for Cambodian football a generation ago.
Stadiums are imperfect indicators of sporting success, but excellent indicators of political ambition. Governments rarely build large stadiums for ticket revenue; they build them to signal modernity, capacity, and relevance.
I have always thought Southeast Asia is one of the world’s most underrated football regions. Not because the football is perfect – it isn’t – but because the relationship between football and society is unusually visible.
Take the away fans. In Europe, travelling supporters are taken for granted. In ASEAN, they tell a different story. Millions of people move around the region for work. A Vietnamese worker in Singapore, a Cambodian worker in Thailand, an Indonesian family in Malaysia – these migration patterns create ready-made football communities. When Indonesia play away, they often find supporters already living in the host country. The tournament becomes less a series of international matches and more a map of Southeast Asian mobility.
Vietnam arrive as the defending champion and look like the region’s most coherent football project. Under Kim Sang-sik, the former South Korean World Cup player, Vietnam has done something unusual in Asian football: it has connected the senior team and the under-23 programme. The results have been almost suspiciously consistent: the 2024 ASEAN title, 2025 ASEAN under-23 success, 2026 SEA Games gold, and a third-place finish in the AFC under-23 championship. It feels less like a golden generation appearing by accident and more like an institution taking shape.
Thailand remains the old empire. It still carries the aura of the region’s historical power, much as Brazil does in South America even during less dominant periods. But empires rarely notice when the gap begins to close.
READ | Football is simple for complete midfielder Rodri
Indonesia is the country that fascinates everyone in football. The population is enormous, the fan culture is volcanic, and the commercial upside is obvious. A recent goal celebration by former Oxford United youngster Marselino Ferdinan against Saudi Arabia went viral globally. That may sound trivial, but in modern football, a celebration becoming a meme is extremely valuable. It means the moment has escaped sport and entered culture.
Singapore is the counterargument to population determinism. They recently qualified for the 2027 AFC Asian Cup ahead of much larger countries, including India and Bangladesh. Under Gavin Lee and Hariss Harun, Singapore has become a reminder that organisation can occasionally outperform demographics.
naturalisation.
Indonesia, Vietnam, Cambodia, Singapore, and Malaysia have increasingly used foreign-born or naturalised players. In Southeast Asia, where dual citizenship is often unavailable for sporting purposes, players frequently have to give up their original nationality. That makes each decision more consequential than many observers realise.
The interesting question is not whether these players are “truly” national – football has always been shaped by migration – but whether the federation has built an environment compelling enough for someone to change their legal identity for it.
Malaysia’s recent administrative crisis over naturalisation shows how quickly the issue can become political.
FIFA’s proposed ASEAN Cup later this year, with invited teams including India and, at least initially, China. The project remains hazy, but its existence raises a classic football question: what happens when a competition that grew organically suddenly finds itself sharing space with a globally branded version of itself?
READ | FIFA final: Spain’s absolute domination over Argentina explained
This is where the current ASEAN Championship becomes more interesting than its marketing suggests. For three decades it has grown because Southeast Asian audiences cared about it, not because anyone told them to or linked development funding to it. The colourful banners, packed away sections, loud anthems, and shirt-wearing commuters on match day were created by supporters before they were monetised by sponsors.
Indian fans should probably pay attention. For years, they stayed awake at impossible hours to watch Europe. The ASEAN Championship offers something different: international football in a friendly time zone, free on YouTube, and taking place in countries that are geographically close but often absent from Indian football conversations.
Today, when the first whistle blows in Phnom Penh, most of the global football industry will still be analysing Spain’s press against Argentina. Meanwhile, Southeast Asia will be doing what it has quietly done for thirty years: turning football into a regional language.
And the rest of the world will eventually discover that the story was happening here all along.
(The writer is the co-founder of SkaSports Investments Pvt Ltd and Director of two football clubs in Cambodia and Singapore)

title: Commonwealth Games 2026: Lovlina assures India its first medal
content:
Lovlina Borgohain assured India of their first medal at the Commonwealth Games after the boxer received a direct entry into the semifinals of the women's 75kg event, which also guaranteed her at least a bronze.
round bye in the five-boxer draw, automatically securing a place in the last four and assuring herself a podium finish.
Lovlina will take on Tuvalu's TKBP Taafaki in the semifinal on July 31, with the winner advancing to the gold medal bout.
This is the first Commonwealth Games medal for the 28-year-old — the only major multi-sport honour that had eluded her decorated career until now.
The Assam boxer has already won medals at the Olympic Games, World Championships, Asian Games and Asian Championships.
Now, she can look forward to upgrading the guaranteed bronze to a gold in Glasgow.

title: CWG 2026: India's complete schedule, timings and medal events on Day 2
content:
Following a vibrant opening ceremony, the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow move into their second day with Indian athletes set to compete across five sports, including several medal events. India's biggest medal hopes on Friday will come in para powerlifting, while gymnasts, boxers, swimmers and lawn bowls players will also begin or continue their campaigns.
Here's the complete schedule for Indian athletes on Day 2 of the Commonwealth Games.
Men's Lightweight Final  - 5:30 PM IST- Ashok Kumar Malik
Men's Team Final & Individual Qualification - 5:00 PM IST- Tapan Mohanty
3:30 PM IST- Preeti Pawar
- Jadumani Singh vs Aaron Cullen (Scotland) - 11:00 PM IST
Men's S13 100m Freestyle Heats - 3:40 PM ISTR.V.V.B.K. Budigna and Imam Ali
Men's 50m Backstroke Heats -  3:56 PM ISTSrihari Natraj
Women's Pairs Section Match - 7:30 PM ISTIndia vs South Africa - Pinki Singh & Rupa Rani Tirkey
Men's Singles Section Match -  10:20 PM ISTPutul Sonowal vs Cecil Alexander (Falkland Islands)
When and Where to watch Commonwealth Games Live on TV and online?
Fans in India can watch the Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games live on the Sony Sports Network, while live streaming will be available on the Sony LIV app and website. The multi-sport event runs from July 23 to August 2, 2026.

title: 'My last match was World Cup final': Otamendi announces international retirement
content:
Argentina defender Nicolas Otamendi has announced his retirement from international football, ending a remarkable 17-year career with the national team.
The 38-year-old leaves as one of Argentina's most successful defenders, having won the 2022 FIFA World Cup and two Copa America titles. His final appearance came in Argentina's 1-0 defeat to Spain in the FIFA World Cup final in the United States.
Otamendi made 139 appearances for Argentina and was a key figure in the team's success over the years. After the final, he shared an emotional message on Instagram, thanking the country for giving him the chance to achieve his biggest dream.
"Today I have to write the most difficult words of my career. As fate would have it, my last match was a World Cup final. It wasn't the result we wanted, but I'm leaving with my head held high, knowing that this team gave it their all right up to the very last second. Thank you, Argentina, for allowing me to fulfil the dream of becoming a world champion," he wrote.
Argentina's hopes of defending their World Cup title ended with a narrow extra-time loss to Spain in the final.
The team had shown great fighting spirit throughout the tournament, recovering from two goals down to beat Egypt in the Round of 32 before staging another dramatic comeback against England in the semi-finals.
However, Spain proved too strong in the final and ended Argentina's reign as world champions.
Decorated career comes to an end
Otamendi also recently returned to Argentina to join River Plate after a successful club career in Europe. During his time abroad, he played for Porto, Valencia, Manchester City and Benfica, winning several major honours.
Having represented Argentina at four FIFA World Cups, Otamendi retires as one of the country's most experienced and accomplished defenders, leaving behind a legacy built on consistency, leadership and success at the highest level.

title: Durand Cup set to introduce women's tournament from next edition
content:
The oldest football tournament in Asia — Durand Cup — will host its 135th edition when Mohun Bagan Super Giant and East Bengal FC take the field at Salt Lake Stadium in the first derby of the season on Saturday.
However, in the 134 years of the tournament’s existence, Indian women’s football has grown by leaps and bounds, and at the culmination of Durand Cup Trophy Tour in the city on Wednesday, the organisers of the prestigious competition — the Indian Armed Forces — revealed their enthusiasm to include a women’s competition in the coming years.
At the ceremonial function and news conference at AOI Vijay Durg, where the tournament’s three iconic trophies were showcased, Major General Dinesh Kumar Singh, vice-chairman of Durand Cup Organising Committee, said they were mulling the introduction of a women’s tournament alongside the men’s event in the near future.
"Why leave the girls behind?" was the question on their minds six months ago when they began preparations for the upcoming season, Singh said.
“So, this is something that we have already thought about. The issue is how many venues and what kind of facilities (will be available) for it. So probably in the next edition we may have a truncated or a smaller version of the girls’ event,” he added.
To add to the expectations, a special women's exhibition match was organised jointly by the Indian Armed Forces and the govt of Manipur at the SAI Ground in Takyel, Imphal, in the build-up to the main competition.
This time, the tournament will see 24 men’s teams compete across five venues, with Ranchi as the newest addition and the Assam matches returning to Guwahati after two editions in Kokrajhar.
“We saw the enthusiasm among the people in Kokrajhar. But the state government wanted it back in Guwahati for ease of organisation,” said Lieutenant General Gambhir Singh, Chief of Staff, Headquarters Eastern Command, and chairman of the Organising Committee.
Among the dignitaries at the event was chief guest Dr Indranil Khan, Minister of State (Independent Charge) for the Departments of Youth Services and Sports and Consumer Affairs in the West Bengal government.
FIFA World Cup, we all supported clubs from around the world. Now it is time to unite behind Indian football and our own footballers,” said Khan.
Meanwhile, Ranchi will host its first-ever Durand Cup match on Sunday, when Jamshedpur FC take on Defenders FC at Birsa Munda Stadium. Defending champions
NorthEast United FC will begin their title defence against Bodoland FC at Guwahati’s Indira Gandhi Athletic Stadium on Aug 1. The other cities hosting the premier competition are the football-crazy northeastern cities of Shillong and Imphal.

title: 'Replay the final': More than 88,000 Argentina fans call for replay after bitter World Cup final defeat
content:
Spain's dramatic 1-0 extra-time victory over Argentina in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final may have settled the destination of the trophy, but the controversy surrounding the showpiece continues to gather momentum days after the final whistle.
A petition demanding that the World Cup final be replayed has now attracted 88,915 signatures at the time of writing, with thousands of Argentina supporters claiming the match was influenced by controversial refereeing decisions.
The online campaign, launched on Change.org by Argentine supporter Gisela Sánchez, calls on FIFA to review the officiating performance of Slovenian referee Slavko Vinčić, who oversaw Sunday's final at the New York New Jersey Stadium.
Petition accuses referee of "controversial and corrupt" performance
Spain secured their second men's World Cup title courtesy of Ferran Torres' 106th-minute winner after extra time, ending Argentina's hopes of defending the trophy they won four years earlier.
However, the result has not been accepted by a section of Argentina supporters.
petition, Sánchez alleges that Vinčić delivered a "controversial and corrupt" performance and urges FIFA to investigate several key decisions made during the final.
"The recent final between Argentina and Spain was tarnished by the controversial and corrupt performance of the referee who, according to video evidence, was bought to influence the match's outcome," the petition states.
"It is a monumental disappointment for the players, coaches, and, above all, for the fans who crave to see a game decided by talent and dedication on the pitch, and not by rigged refereeing decisions.
"If we allow corruption to go unnoticed on this global stage, we are setting a bad example for future generations of athletes and fans.
"Therefore, we ask FIFA to review these incidents with seriousness and transparency, and to consider replaying the final, this time without the influence of a corrupt referee."
88,000 Argentina fans demand World Cup final replay after 'controversial and corrupt' referee claims
The petition, however, does not provide independently verified evidence to support those allegations beyond videos circulating across social media which the organiser claims favour Spain.
Spain dominated before sealing victory in extra time
Despite the reaction from some supporters, Spain were widely viewed as the better side throughout the final.
Goalkeeper Emiliano Martínez repeatedly kept Argentina in the contest before Ferran Torres finally broke the deadlock in the 106th minute of extra time.
Fans watch the World Cup final soccer match between Argentina and Spain in Buenos Aires, Argentina, Sunday, July 19, 2026. (AP Photo/Gustavo Garello)
Argentina's task had already become significantly harder after midfielder Enzo Fernández was sent off in the third minute of stoppage time at the end of normal time.
kick, Fernández received a second yellow card after a reckless challenge on Spain defender Pau Cubarsí, reducing Lionel Scaloni's side to ten men before extra time began.
According to match statistics, Vinčić awarded 46 fouls during the final, with 25 committed by Argentina as the match became increasingly fragmented.
Tempers boiled over after the final whistle
The frustration carried over into the closing moments of the evening.
Immediately after Spain were confirmed as world champions, ugly scenes erupted on the pitch.
Leandro Paredes was sent off after the final whistle following clashes involving Eric García and Gavi, while Nahuel Molina also appeared to spark part of the confrontation after confronting Spain captain Rodri.
The incidents have since become the subject of a separate FIFA disciplinary review.
Speaking during ITV's coverage, former England defender Gary Neville strongly criticised Argentina's conduct.
"It's a disgrace," Neville said.
"I love the competitiveness of the Argentines, fighting and scrapping for their lives every game. Messi's propped them up at times and they're very competitive and I love that element of them.
"But it's been a disgrace the last few games."
Similar replay campaign emerged earlier in the tournament
The Argentina petition is not the first online campaign calling for a World Cup replay during this tournament.
Following Spain's semi-final victory over France, French supporters launched a similar Change.org petition after disputing a controversial incident that led to the penalty opening the scoring.
That campaign also gathered more than 82,000 signatures, although it had no effect on FIFA's official result.
Likewise, there is no indication that the latest petition will alter the outcome of the World Cup final.
Meanwhile, Change.org has also hosted petitions taking the opposite position. One campaign calling for Argentina to be banned from future World Cups amassed more than 29 million signatures before eventually being closed.
Away from the online debate, thousands of Argentina supporters gathered across the country following the team's return home, applauding Lionel Scaloni's squad for reaching a second consecutive World Cup final despite falling just short of retaining their title against an impressive Spanish side.

title: Best of best: FIFA unveils World Cup Dream XI with 11 players voted as tournament's finest
content:
Spain may have lifted the 2026 FIFA World Cup trophy after defeating Argentina 1-0 following extra time in New Jersey, but the tournament's best-performing players have now also been recognised after FIFA revealed its official Dream XI.
The Team of the Tournament, selected through a global fan vote, reflects the standout performers from six weeks of football across the United States, Canada and Mexico. While Spain, France and Argentina dominate the selection after reaching the semi-finals, England's third-place finish was rewarded with just a single representative.
Jude Bellingham is the only England player to make FIFA's Dream XI despite Thomas Tuchel's side ending the tournament on a high with a remarkable 6-4 victory over France in the third-place play-off.
Bellingham's inclusion comes after another outstanding major tournament for the Real Madrid midfielder.
The 23-year-old finished with seven goals, the highest tally ever recorded by an England player at a single FIFA World Cup, helping the Three Lions secure their best finish since winning the tournament in 1966.
Despite England producing several standout performers, including Bukayo Saka's historic hat-trick against France and Declan Rice's record-breaking third-place play-off display, Bellingham was the only English player voted into the final XI.
Spain, France and Argentina dominate selections
Fresh from captaining Spain to their second World Cup title, Rodri headlines the midfield after also winning the Golden Ball as the tournament's best player.
Spain have three representatives in total, with both full-backs Marc Cucurella and Pedro Porro joining their captain in the side after Luis de la Fuente's men completed an unbeaten tournament by defeating Argentina in the final.
France also supply three players despite finishing fourth.
Golden Boot winner Kylian Mbappé occupies the right side of the attack after finishing the tournament with 10 goals, becoming the first player to win the World Cup Golden Boot twice while also breaking Lionel Messi's all-time World Cup scoring record with 22 career goals.
breaking campaign of his own. The France playmaker finished the tournament with seven assists, the most ever recorded by a player at a single FIFA World Cup.
Dayot Upamecano completes France's contingent in central defence.
Argentina, beaten finalists after Ferran Torres' 106th-minute winner, have two players in the XI.
Lionel Messi features on the left wing after finishing the tournament with eight goals and four assists, while defender Lisandro Martínez partners Upamecano at the heart of the defence.
Completing the front three is Norway striker Erling Haaland, who marked his World Cup debut by firing his country to a historic first-ever quarter-final appearance while scoring seven goals during the tournament.
Cape Verde cult hero earns goalkeeper spot
Perhaps the biggest surprise in the Dream XI is the goalkeeper selection.
Rather than Golden Glove winner Unai Simón, England goalkeeper Jordan Pickford or France's Mike Maignan, fans voted for Cape Verde veteran Vozinha.
The 40-year-old became one of the breakout stars of the tournament during Cape Verde's memorable run to the Round of 32 before they narrowly lost 3-2 after extra time to eventual finalists Argentina.
Vozinha kept clean sheets against both Spain and Saudi Arabia, made 18 saves across the tournament and outperformed his expected goals against figure by 0.9.
His performances also made him an unlikely social media sensation. According to tournament figures, the goalkeeper began Cape Verde's opening match against Spain with around 50,000 Instagram followers. After producing seven saves against the eventual champions and continuing his impressive campaign, that number reportedly surged to 29.5 million by the end of the World Cup.
FIFA World Cup 2026 Dream XI
The Dream XI rounds off the individual honours following the conclusion of the tournament.
Rodri claimed the Golden Ball as the competition's best player after leading Spain to the title, with Messi and Mbappé receiving the Silver Ball and Bronze Ball respectively.
Mbappé finished as Golden Boot winner with 10 goals, edging Messi, who ended the tournament on eight goals and four assists, while Spain goalkeeper Unai Simón received the Golden Glove after conceding just one goal in eight matches. Teenage defender Pau Cubarsí collected the FIFA Young Player Award, and the Netherlands were recognised with the FIFA Fair Play Award after receiving just three yellow cards throughout their campaign.
